headline: RMAF Fighter Fleet Down to Only 18 Operational Aircraft
context: RMAF has only 18 operational fighter aircraft out of 36 fleet due to maintenance issues and MiG-29 grounding.

cards.big: RMAF down to 18 operational fighters out of 36 fleet total
cards.sub: All 8 MiG-29N permanently grounded leaving Su-30MKM and FA-18D only

cards.big: 8 MiG-29N grounded since 2020 with no reactivation or replacement plans
cards.sub: Russian sanctions and spare parts embargo made operation impossible

cards.big: Su-30MKM fleet of 18 has only 10 operational due to engine delays
cards.sub: AL-31F engine overhauls require 18-month turnaround at Russian facilities

cards.big: Light Combat Aircraft procurement estimated at RM4-6 billion
cards.sub: Decision between FA-50 and JF-17 expected by end of 2026

cards.big: Fighter readiness tests whether air defence capability matches regional security needs
Only 18 operational RMAF fighters — air sovereignty requires a fleet large enough to maintain readiness.
